repository_state.head: 4fe10683ed4e4ecb85482c7a7a71dc08a84d4a83
repository_state.note: This freezes dataset scope, not the still-changing NarratorDB implementation. The exact implementation commit must be frozen in a later protocol amendment before any scored run.

state.reason: Reader, judge, provider, retry, adapter, implementation, and scalar success rules are not frozen yet.

claim_semantics.allowed_label: BEAM-1M dataset scope precommitted before a NarratorDB BEAM-1M development or evaluation run; locally unrun by NarratorDB based on the repository audit; public-answer and not globally hidden.
eligible_as: a direct public-benchmark comparison with systems evaluated on the same BEAM-1M tier after protocol alignment
not_eligible_as: a globally hidden or secret test
proof that no BEAM-1M answer-bearing artifact ever existed anywhere in the local environment
an official BEAM score until the exact official-compatible answerer and evaluator protocol is frozen and completed

dataset_scope.selection: all conversations in the official 1M tier

source_file.sha256_provenance: Official Hugging Face tree metadata lfs.oid; Git LFS object IDs are the SHA-256 of file content.

dataset_scope.immutability_rule: The tier, full 35-conversation denominator, dataset revision, source path, and source SHA-256 cannot be changed by a later amendment.

access_attestation.scope: Actions in this precommit work session plus repository-visible files; this is not an audit of browser caches, provider systems, other machines, or human memory.
metadata_inspected: official Hugging Face dataset-card and tree metadata
local filenames, sizes, hashes, binary-text matches, and Git history without opening result payloads

prior_local_use_audit.audit_scope: Current repository tree including ignored files, binary-text filename matches, and reachable Git path/content history at precommit time.

known_narratordb_beam_use.scope: one conversation and one question per ability
known_narratordb_beam_use.claim_status: retrieval diagnostic only; not an official BEAM pass rate

prior_local_use_audit.conclusion: No NarratorDB BEAM-1M run or official 1M parquet was found, but pre-existing vendor result files prevent an unqualified locally-unseen claim.

verified_official_evaluator_behavior.per_conversation_aggregation: For each ability, report_results.py averages question scores; event_ordering uses tau_norm and the other abilities use llm_judge_score.
verified_official_evaluator_behavior.tier_aggregation: For each ability, report_results.py takes the arithmetic mean of the per-conversation values across included conversation directories.
verified_official_evaluator_behavior.note: The official report script emits ten ability columns. This artifact does not invent a single scalar or a 95% success rule.

requirements: A later hash-stable protocol artifact must reference this artifact and its published SHA-256.
Every unresolved field must be frozen before any NarratorDB BEAM-1M development or scored run.
The later artifact may not change the frozen dataset scope.
Any failed, timed-out, or malformed item must remain in the denominator under the frozen failure policy.

third_party_validation_requirements: Fetch only the pinned official dataset revision and verify the 1M parquet SHA-256 before evaluation.
Run all 35 conversations with no sampling or post-hoc exclusions.
Keep reference answers and rubrics outside the NarratorDB process and expose only conversation inputs and probing questions.
Record immutable input/output hashes, provider request identifiers, timestamps, retries, failures, token usage, and costs.
Release raw system outputs and evaluator outputs so another party can deterministically re-score them.
Report that hosted-model generation may not be bit-for-bit reproducible and publish the exact rerun policy before execution.
